sourceMovie.title: Krampus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Krampus (2015) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gremlins
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Krampus clicked for you, Gremlins (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Violent Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Krampus clicked for you, Violent Night (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Krampus?
faq.answer: Try Gremlins, Violent Night, Trick 'r Treat—each shares DNA with Krampus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Krampus?
faq.answer: Krampus (2015) is best described as Horror, Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Krampus worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
